title: Subcloning colonial ascidians
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1kpsk","text":"We have been subcloning colonial ascidians since some time already, till now - with a lot of success. This protocol has been ruinously used by many of our co-workers.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"3m7nq","text":"2 	containers with system water
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fvco8","text":"1 	plastic tray
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cb33b","text":"1 	paintbrush
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1ij4i","text":"1 	microfiber tissue
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bkdto","text":"1 	single-edge razor blade (sharp)
","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"22d49","text":"numbered glass slides and plastic slide racks (as many as required)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Place the cleaned slides to be subclone in the first tray inside a slide rack. Cleaning colonial ascidians by Marta Wawrzyniak
Take one slide.
Decide how many additional slides you want to propagate your colony onto.

Subcloning is typically performed either to recenter systems onto the same slide, to provide more space to large colonies by propagating them onto new slides, or to split a strain into multiple clones.
Systems from the same clone can be regrouped during subcloning, they will then fuse into one larger clone.
Wet the new slides with system water, let them dry upwards in the plastic tray.
Using a sharp razor blade, amputate the systems to be moved.
If systems need to be split or separated from the rest of the colony, perform the incision first using a gentle vertical slicing movement.
Detach the systems to be subcloned by performing a horizontal slicing movement on the glass slide. The system should come to rest on the razor blade.
If needed, slice the excess and/or dirty tunic underneath the subclone to provide a flat attachment surface.
Take the target slide, dry it, place it horizontally in front of you.
Transfer the subclone onto the new slide.
Gently push the subclone from the surface of the razor blade using a wet paintbrush.
If the subclone is too small to be pushed out of the razor blade, place it on the paintbrush by performing a rolling movement, deposit it on the slide by perform the converse movement.
Dry the excess water around the subclone.
Look underneath the subclone, make sure there is no air bubble trapped under it.
Let the subclone dry vertically in the plastic tray for 00:05:00 .
Using the paintbrush, gently wet the zooids of the subclone. Make sure all tissue gets wet properly
Dry the excess water and let it dry once more for 5 min.
Repeat steps 12 and 13
Let it rest for 00:30:00 .
Take all the subclones slides out of the water, place a maximum of 7 per slide rack, put the whole rack vertically in the quarantine.
Check all subclones, clean the dead tissue off the slides.
Move the subcloned colonies to the main system.
Dry the excess water on the colony using the microfiber tissue.
Put the subclone upside down on your fingers.
Hold it gently with your thumb.
Slice the excess tunic perpendicularly
Take the slide, enter the water diagonally and place it into the second container vertically.
If the subclone detaches from the slide, start again from 12.
Typical issues are a dirty tunic (repeat step 5.3) and dried tissue (repeat step 12).
Let it rest for 48:00:00 .
creator: Marta Wawrzyniak
